sections.paragraph:
Ῥόδος, ου, ἡ (Hom. et al.; ins; 1 Macc 15:23; Philo, Aet. M. 120; Joseph.; SibOr 3, 444) Rhodes, an island off the southwest point of Asia Minor; its main city bears the same name. Ac 21:1.—Pauly-W., Suppl. V, 731–840; Kl. Pauly, 1421–23; BHHW III 1596. PECS 755–58.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

PECS PECS = Princeton Encyclopedia of Classical Sites—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 907
